messages.content:
One way to increase pressure on a gas is to
Group of answer choices

increase the number of gas particles

hold all other variables constant

decrease the temperature

lower the kinetic energy of the particles

increase the volume of the container
increase the number of gas particles

If pressure is held constant, the relationship between temperature and volume is
Group of answer choices

directly proportional

inversely proportional

unrelated

exponentially proportional
directly proportional

If temperature is constant, the relationship between pressure and volume is
Group of answer choices

unrelated

directly proportional

exponentially proportional

inversely proportional
inversely proportional

According to kinetic-molecular theory, which of these describes a gas?
Group of answer choices

large particles in constant, random motion

small particles in constant, random motion

large particles far apart in uniform motion

small particles far apart in uniform motion
small particles in constant, random motion

A gas that is confined in a rigid container is heated. Which of the following statements is true?
Group of answer choices

the pressure will increase

the volume will increase

the density will decrease

the kinetic energy of the particles will decrease

the number of moles of gas will increase
The pressure will increase

A closed, flexible container (e.g., a balloon) maintains constant pressure and number of moles of gas (i.e. it does not leak.) If the volume of the container is decreased, the temperature will
Group of answer choices

decrease for a few seconds, then increase slowly.

decrease.

increase for a few seconds, then decrease slowly.

remain the same.

increase.
increase.

When using the celsius temperature scale is is possible to have negative temperatures, but on the kelvin scale no temperature is lower than 0. How is it beneficial to use kelvin when performing gas law calculations?
Group of answer choices

Kelvin scale uses absolute zero as the lowest temperature so you can be far more accurate when performing calculations with it.

Kelvin scale uses easier numbers for calculations.

Using delving is only important because the ideal gas constant uses kelvin and units must match

Using a negative temperature on the celsius scale could cause a negative temperature, volume, pressure or amount of gas which would not make sense.
Kelvin scale uses absolute zero as the lowest temperature so you can be far more accurate when performing calculations with it.

A rigid metal tank contains helium gas. Which applies to the gas in the tank when some helium is removed at constant temperature?
Group of answer choices

the number of moles of gas particles remains constant

the volume of the gas decreases

the pressure of the gas decreases

the average speed of the gas molecules decreases

the force of the gas molecules on the sides of the container increases
the number of moles of gas particles remains constant

The pressure of a gas will _____ when the volume is decreased and will ______ when the absolute temperature is decreased.
Group of answer choices

stay constant, decrease

increase, decrease

decrease, decrease

increase, increase

increase, stay constant
increase, decrease

If the volume of a sample of Cl2 gas is tripled while the pressure is held constant, what happens to the absolute temperature?
Group of answer choices

It doubles.

It decreases by 1/6.

It remains constant.

It triples.

It increases 9 times.

It decreases by 1/3.
It remains constant.

If the temperature of a fixed quantity and volume of gas changes, what also changes?
Group of answer choices

formula

moles

pressure

mass

volume
pressure
